payload.title: Upgrade voice transcription adapter to use Narada credential references instead of env-only token lookup
payload.summary: The operator-surface voice intake is blocked because the Cloudflare/Harmonia transcription adapter only sees HARMONIA_VOICE_TRANSCRIPTION_TOKEN in the process environment. Narada already has doctrine for credential references and Windows Credential Manager precedence, but the voice adapter is still env-only. Upgrade it to resolve an admitted credential reference through Narada's credential/capability model.
observed_behavior: Live microphone capture succeeds and emits voice state events consumed by YASB.
Cloudflare transcription adapter fails closed when HARMONIA_VOICE_TRANSCRIPTION_TOKEN is missing from the process environment.
Narada docs already define credential references and Windows Credential Manager/env/.env precedence, but this adapter does not use that machinery.
requested_upgrade: Allow the voice transcription adapter to accept a credential reference, not only HARMONIA_VOICE_TRANSCRIPTION_TOKEN from inherited env.
Support at least env: and credential-manager: references for Windows User/PC Site operation.
Use fail-closed behavior with an operator-visible repair message when the credential reference cannot be resolved.
Record/read capability grant evidence for remote_audio_transcription or equivalent capability before remote audio is sent.
Keep raw token values out of config, logs, traces, YASB state, and recognition result artifacts.
Expose doctor/preflight output that distinguishes microphone availability from transcription credential availability.
acceptance_criteria: With token stored in Windows Credential Manager and no process env var, voice transcription succeeds or reaches the remote worker with authorized request.
With no credential available, YASB and runtime state report blocked with exact credential-ref repair guidance.
No raw token appears in artifacts or logs.
Mic capture without remote transcription remains usable for debugging.
